_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Biliary tract obstruction or disease requiring diversion
clinical_indications: Retained, recurrent, and impacted bile duct stones
Stenosis of the sphincter of Oddi
Biliary obstruction, either benign or malignant

clinical_thresholds.note: Dilated common bile duct shown on CT Abdomen / ERCP / MRCP

preauth.label: Clinical Notes with history and examination and planned line of treatment
LFT with Bilirubin, RFT, Coagulation Profile and Serum Electrolytes
CT Abdomen / Endoscopic Retrograde Cholangiopancreatography (ERCP) / Magnetic resonance cholangiopancreatography (MRCP) showing dilated (>1.3 cm) common bile duct

ppd_preauth.q: Were the patient's clinical history/investigations indicative of the Procedure?
Whether the investigation reports confirm the diagnosis?

common_queries: Patient's clinical history or investigations are not indicative of the procedure.
Investigation reports do not confirm the diagnosis.
CT Abdomen / ERCP / MRCP report is missing or does not show a common bile duct dilated to >1.3 cm.
Mandatory pre-authorization documents (Clinical Notes, LFT/RFT/Coagulation Profile/Serum Electrolytes) are missing or incomplete.
The procedure was documented as performed along with other stone removal procedures, such as CBD exploration.
Detailed Operative/Procedure notes are missing or incomplete at the claim submission stage.
Detailed Discharge Summary is missing or incomplete at the claim submission stage.
Mandatory claim submission documents (Detailed Indoor Case Papers, Post Procedure LFT, Intra Operative Clinical Photograph) are missing or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: Choledochoduodenostomy and Choledochojejunostomy are used in the treatment of selected patients with retained, recurrent, and impacted bile duct stones; strictures of bile ducts; stenosis of the sphincter of Oddi; pancreatitis associated with biliary disease, choledochal cysts, fistulas of bile duct; and biliary obstruction, either benign or malignant.
The procedure is not done along with other stone removal procedures, such as CBD exploration.

Choledochoduodenostomy: A right subcostal incision is usually performed. The duodenum is widely mobilised by a generous Kocher manoeuvre so that it can be approximated to the common bile duct without tension. A 2.0-2.5 cm longitudinal incision is made in the distal common bile duct, as close as possible to the area of stenosis or obstruction in patients with benign disease. In patients with unresectable cancer of the duct or pancreas, the bile duct opening should be placed high in the duct. In patients with stricture, the bile duct is divided, and the stricture is excised. The duodenum and duct are joined by a posterior row of interrupted 3-0 silk sutures. The duodenum is opened longitudinally for 2.0-2.5 cm, and the second row of interrupted 3-0 or 4-0 chromic catgut sutures are placed with thin-walled ducts or different anastomosis. A final row of interrupted 3-0 silk sutures completes the anterior row of anastomosis.

Choledochojejunostomy: A right subcostal incision is again preferred since it gives optimal exposure to the subhepatic area in most patients. The bile duct is exposed, and a longitudinal, 2.0-2.5 cm opening is made in a distal duct for benign obstruction, in the upper duct for patients with unresectable malignant disease, or duct is divided for patients with a complete stricture. A Roux-en-Y jejunal segment is prepared, the end of the jejunal limb is closed, and the jejunum approximated to the bile duct with a posterior row of interrupted 3-0 silk sutures. The jejunum is opened longitudinally for 2.0-2.5 cm, and a second inner row of interrupted 3-0 or 4-0 chromic catgut sutures is placed. A T-tube may be used for selected patients with difficult anastomoses. A final row of interrupted 3-0 silk sutures completes the anastomoses.
contraindications: Procedure is not done along with other stone removal procedures, such as CBD exploration.
